items.front: Where are the procedures in Chapter 10 Section 6 to be used?
What are the three emergency phases under 10-6-2 and their abbreviations?
items.back: 1. Uncertainty phase (INCERFA) — concern about safety. 2. Alert phase (ALERFA) — apprehension about safety. 3. Distress phase (DETRESFA) — reasonable certainty of grave and imminent danger.

items.front: When does an INCERFA exist under 10-6-2a?
items.back: When no communication received within 30 minutes after the time a communication should have been received or after the first unsuccessful attempt, whichever is earlier. Or aircraft fails to arrive within 30 minutes of last ETA, whichever is later.

items.front: When does an ALERFA exist under 10-6-2b?
items.back: When uncertainty-phase inquiries fail to reveal information, OR information indicates operating efficiency impaired (but not forced landing likely), OR no communication within 60 minutes of expected contact.

items.front: When does a DETRESFA exist under 10-6-2c?
items.back: When alert-phase inquiries fail, OR fuel exhausted/insufficient to reach safety, OR information indicates forced landing is likely, OR reasonably certain the aircraft is about to make or has made a forced landing.

items.front: What three categories of aircraft receive alerting service under 10-6-3a?
items.back: 1. All aircraft receiving ATC service. 2. All other aircraft that filed a flight plan or are otherwise known to ATC. 3. Any aircraft known or believed to be subject to unlawful interference.

items.front: What three responsibilities does the Area Control Center serve as control point for under 10-6-3c?
items.back: 1. Collecting all information relevant to a state of emergency. 2. Forwarding that information to the appropriate RCC. 3. Coordinating with other facilities concerned.

items.front: What nine items must INCERFA/ALERFA/DETRESFA messages include under 10-6-3g?
items.back: 1. Phase (INCERFA/ALERFA/DETRESFA). 2. Agency/person originating. 3. Nature of emergency. 4. Significant flight plan info. 5. Last radio contact unit/time/freq. 6. Last position report. 7. Color/distinctive marks. 8. Action taken. 9. Other remarks.

items.front: What phraseology is used when traffic prevents an unrestricted emergency descent under 10-6-4?
items.back: 'ATC ADVISES (aircraft identification) UNABLE TO APPROVE UNRESTRICTED DESCENT. TRAFFIC (traffic information). REQUEST INTENTIONS.'

items.front: What information must an emergency descent broadcast include under 10-6-4c4?
items.back: Location of emergency descent, direction of flight, type aircraft, route if appropriate, altitude vacated, and other information.

items.front: When may IFR separation be discontinued between a SAR aircraft and a distressed aircraft under 10-6-5?
items.back: When visual or radar contact has been established by the SAR aircraft AND the pilots of both aircraft concur.

items.front: Should a SAR aircraft be cleared to the distressed aircraft's position as a fix under 10-6-5?
items.back: No. Clear to a fixed clearance limit, not to the distressed aircraft (which is a moving fix). Issue route clearances consistent with the distressed aircraft's route.
